Mr. [REDACTED], an artist living at [REDACTED], Chicago, Illinois ([REDACTED]) advised SA [REDACTED] on March 11, 1952 that he saw a flying disc at 9:00 AM on March 6, 1952.

[REDACTED] stated that he was looking out of a window at his home which window faces south, when he saw a flying disc at approximately 7,000 feet above Fullerton Avenue. The angle of elevation of the disc above the horizon was about 45 degrees. The disc came out of a cloud in the east, stopped and hung motionless in mid-air for a split second, then flew due south at great speed.
[REDACTED] made a sketch of the disc and the sketch and the above information were furnished to the local office of the Office of Special Investigations.

Alas Air Comd reports military jet pilots via
Fairbanks while over Weeks International Field at an altitude
distance to object was aprx 50 miles. Soon after flash a
dark brown smoke appeared to [unclear] or climb at an angle of
40 degrees. At the leading edge of the smoke at aprx 100
distance of aprx 50 to 55,000 feet. Color of smoke brown,
sun. One pilot had object in view aprx 4½ mins. First
sighted at 150026Z. Coordinates 64 degs 13 mins North, 149
degs 30 mins West. Lis info will be rvd when obtained.

DETECTION OF UNIDENTIFIED OBJCXXX OBJECTS OVER OAK RIDGE AREA, PROTECTION
IN ATOXXX ATMOSRHERE. CONDUCT APPROPRIATE INVESTIGATION TO DETERMINE
ORIGINAL COPY FILED IN
APPLICATIONS SECTION
NINETEEN FIFTY, PROTECTION OF VITAL INSTALLATIONS. USAF RADAR INSTALL-
ATION AT KNOXVILLE AT ELEVEN TWENTYFIVE PM, EST, OCTOBER TWELVE, PICKED
UP INDICATIONS OF ELEVEN OBJECTS AND PERHAPS MORE TRAVELING ACROSS
CONTROLLED AREA OF ATOMIC ENERGY INSTALLATION AT OAK RIDGE. ALTITUDE
OF OBJECTS VARIED FROM ONE THOUSAND TO FIVE THOUSAND FEET, COURSES
FROM SOUTH SOUTH EAST TO SOUTHEAST, AND DENSITY FROM READING MADE BY
LIGHT AIRCRAFT TO AIRCRAFT EQUAL IN SIZE TO C FORTY SEVEN, SPEED
FROM ONE HUNDRED TO ONE HUNDRED TWENTYFIVE MILES PER HOUR. FIGHTER
PLANS ATTEMPTED TO INTERCEPT IN FEW MINUTES AND RADAR SCREENS REFLECTED
CONTACT, BUT PLANS REPORTED NO OBJECT COULD BE SEEN EITHER VISUALLY
OR ON PLANES RADAR SCREEN. AEC PATROL WAS ALERTED BUT NO OBJECTS COULD
BE VISUALLY SIGHTED BY THEM OVER THE AREA OTHER THAN THE AIR
FORCE INTERCEPTOR. OAK AND EASTERN AIR DEFENSE FORCES HAVE BEEN
ADVISED AND ARE CHECKING INCIDENT. NO REASONABLE EXPLANATION FOR
RADAR READINGS YET DEVELOPED ALTHOUGH OPERATORS ARE EXPERIENCED
RELIABLE PERSONNEL AND RADAR SET IS IN PERFECT OPERATING CONDITION.
BUREAU WILL BE ADVISED OF FURTHER DEVELOPMENTS.
ACK AND HOLD PIS
